name: create-pull-request
tagline: Step-by-step GitHub PR creation skill using the gh CLI — prerequisite checks, context gathering, template-based PR body, and error handling.
verdict: Thorough, safe workflow with strong failure-path and re-run coverage, and gh usage that stays within the user's own repo/auth. One real inconsistency: it detects the actual base branch but then hardcodes --base main in the create command; also lacks negative triggers and a versioning signal.

highlights.summary: No exclusions or 'not for' guidance present anywhere in the skill.
Lines 56-60 discover the real base branch (may be master) but gh pr create at line 159 hardcodes --base main regardless.
No version field, changelog, or dated revision notes anywhere in the skill.
